name: Fredric Hegland

hero.eyebrow: Software engineering, systems curiosity, and a bit of orbit
hero.headline: I like building digital systems that are practical, polished, and quietly ambitious.
hero.description: I am a software engineering master's student who enjoys working across architecture, backend, frontend, AI, infrastructure, and the small details that make a system feel good to use.

quickFacts: Works across backend, frontend, AI, and infrastructure
Enjoys systems that feel practical, polished, and a little playful
currently: Pursuing a joint Master's in Software Engineering (UiB/HVL)
Building enterprise AI systems and chatbot workflows at Amitec AS
Experimenting with self-hosted AI, local inference, and RAG pipelines
interests: AI, machine learning, and quantum computing
Low-level computing, terminals, and efficient tooling
New and old technology with real-world utility
about: I completed a Bachelor's in Computer Technology at the University of Bergen and am now continuing with a joint Master's in Software Engineering. What keeps software interesting for me is the mix of big-picture thinking and small details: architecture, networking, backend logic, UX polish, developer workflow, and how a system feels for the person actually using it.

experience.title: Student Software Engineer
experience.details: Building an enterprise chatbot across the full stack, including architecture, backend, frontend, networking, security, and databases.; Working with AVEVA PI systems (PI AF, PI Vision, PI SMT) and time-series data for energy sector clients.
experience.linkLabel: AVEVA PI systems (PI AF, PI Vision, PI SMT)

experience.title: Sales Co-worker
experience.details: Warehouse and sales department: customer service, stock management and sales.

experience.title: Driver and storage-worker
experience.details: Delivered bread and pastries to private customers and bakeries.

education.program: Joint Master's Program in Software Engineering (with ML courses)

highlights: Bachelor's completed, Master's underway, with a clear interest in systems that are both capable and useful.
Comfortable in small teams where the work crosses multiple layers of the stack.
Hands-on experience with Python, Kotlin, JavaScript/TypeScript, C#/.NET, SQL, NoSQL, Linux, Git, and AI-focused tooling.
Builds course projects and personal experiments to keep learning by doing.
Leadership experience from organizing ITxBergen and supporting the local tech student community.

projects.name: Portfolio Website
projects.description: This site, built with React, Vite, and a custom Three.js background with starfields, galaxies, comets, and scroll-driven camera motion.
projects.highlight: Part portfolio, part UI playground, and a good excuse to keep refining the details.

projects.name: Enterprise Chatbot
projects.description: An enterprise chatbot project where I work across business logic, architecture, networking, databases, and retrieval-augmented AI approaches.
projects.highlight: This is the kind of work I enjoy most: broad systems work with real users and real constraints.

projects.name: Personal AI Homelab
projects.description: A custom local AI agent running on my own hardware with open-source models, vLLM inference, RAG backed by Qdrant, and tool use for search and utilities.
projects.highlight: This is where curiosity turns into tinkering: local models, orchestration, retrieval, and self-hosted infrastructure.

projects.name: Machine Learning Experimentation
projects.description: Experiments around machine learning models, embeddings, quantum computing ideas, and implementing algorithms from scratch to understand them better.

projects.name: Programming Languages and Low-Level Exploration
projects.description: A repository for exploring programming languages and lower-level interests, including RISC-V assembly, x86_64 assembly, C++, Rust, and other systems-oriented experiments.

projects.name: Group Project - Modern Software Development Methods (Kollekt)
projects.description: A full-stack application for shared households, aimed at making everyday coordination a bit easier.

projects.name: Group Project - Advanced Software Technologies
projects.description: A poll application built with several advanced technologies from the course, plus a few extra tools added out of curiosity.

projects.name: Group Project - System Development
projects.description: A group project where we developed a 2D game using LibGDX in Java for a university course, INF112 at UiB.

skills: AI/ML work and applications of AI/ML for both personal use and enterprise

boardPositions.title: ITxBergen Leader
boardPositions.details: Overall responsibility for organizing the annual career day for all IT students in Bergen and other events; administrative decisions and organizational development.

boardPositions.title: ITxBergen Board Member
boardPositions.details: Organizing and planning the annual career day and other minor events for IT students in Bergen.

boardPositions.title: Vektorprogrammet - Assistant
boardPositions.details: Tutor in Mathematics 1P-Y at Danielsen Videregående Skole, Bergen.

contact.note: Always happy to talk about software engineering, AI systems, practical architecture, or simply an interesting technical problem.
